title: Amendment SA 2137 to H.R. 3684
content: Amendment SA 2137 to the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act. This amendment modifies several key provisions of the underlying bill. Section 1: Broadband Modifications. This amendment revises Section 5 of H.R. 3684 to include enhanced provisions for rural broadband deployment. Specifically adds: (a) Rural Priority - At least 25% of broadband funds must be allocated to projects serving rural areas with populations under 20,000. (b) Tribal Lands - Establishes a dedicated $2 billion fund for broadband deployment on tribal lands and in areas serving Native American communities. (c) Speed Requirements - Updates minimum broadband speed requirements to 100 Mbps download and 20 Mbps upload to reflect modern needs. Section 2: Highway Funding Formula Changes. Modifies the highway funding distribution formula referenced in Section 4 to provide additional weight to states with extensive rural road networks and bridges rated in poor condition. Section 3: Buy America Provisions. Strengthens requirements that materials used in infrastructure projects be manufactured in the United States, with limited exceptions for materials not sufficiently available domestically. Section 4: Labor Standards. Ensures that projects funded under this Act comply with prevailing wage requirements and include project labor agreements for large-scale construction projects exceeding $35 million. Section 5: Environmental Review Streamlining. Provides mechanisms to accelerate environmental review processes while maintaining environmental protection standards, enabling faster project implementation.
